sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.52 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). By a famous theorem of Wielandt the sequence$G_0=G,\,G_1,$, where$G_{i+1}={ Aut\,}G_i$, stabilizes for any finite group$G$with trivial centre. Does there exist a function$f$of natural argument such that$|G_i| f(|G|)$for all$i=0,\,1,$for an arbitrary finite group$G$and the same kind of sequence?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
